Ω : Type u_3

μ : Measure Ω
ι : Type u_6
inst✝⁶ : LinearOrder ι
inst✝⁵ : OrderBot ι
inst✝⁴ : Countable ι
inst✝³ : TopologicalSpace ι
inst✝² : OrderTopology ι
inst✝¹ : FirstCountableTopology ι
X : ι → Ω → ℝ
𝓕 : Filtration ι mΩ
inst✝ : SigmaFiniteFiltration μ 𝓕
hX : Submartingale X 𝓕 μ
τ : ℕ → Ω → WithTop ι
hτ : ∀ (i : ℕ), IsStoppingTime 𝓕 (τ i)
n : ι
hτ_le : ∀ (i : ℕ) (ω : Ω), τ i ω ≤ ↑n
⊢ UniformIntegrable (fun i => stoppedValue X (τ i)) 1 μ

let Y : ℕ → Ω → ℝ := fun i ↦ stoppedValue X (τ i)
  have h₁ : ∀ i, IsLIntegrable (Y i) μ := by
    intro i
    have h₂ : IsLIntegrable (X n) μ := by
      have h₃ : IsLIntegrable (X n) μ := by
        -- Use the fact that X n is integrable because it is a martingale step.
        have h₄ : Integrable (X n) (μ := μ) := by
          have h₅ : Integrable (X n) (μ := μ) := by
            -- Use the submartingale property to get integrability.
            have h₆ : Submartingale X 𝓕 μ := hX
            exact h₆.integrable n
          exact h₅
        exact h₄.isLIntegrable
      exact h₃
    have h₃ : IsLIntegrable (Y i) μ := by
      -- Use the fact that Y i is a conditional expectation of X n
      have h₄ : IsLIntegrable (fun ω : Ω ↦ ⨆ k : ℕ, (X n) (ω) : Ω → ℝ) μ := by
        -- This step is a placeholder to show that the supremum of X n is integrable.
        -- In reality, we would need to use the submartingale property to prove this.
        have h₅ : IsLIntegrable (X n) μ := h₂
        have h₆ : IsLIntegrable (fun ω : Ω ↦ ⨆ k : ℕ, (X n) (ω) : Ω → ℝ) μ := by
          -- Use the fact that the supremum of an integrable function is integrable.
          convert h₅ using 1 with ω
          <;> simp [iSup_const]
          <;> norm_num
        exact h₆
      -- Now we can conclude that Y i is integrable.
      have h₅ : IsLIntegrable (Y i) μ := by
        -- Use the fact that Y i is a conditional expectation of a bounded function.
        have h₆ : IsLIntegrable (X n) μ := h₂
        have h₇ : IsLIntegrable (Y i) μ := by
          -- Use the fact that the conditional expectation of an integrable function is integrable.
          have h₈ : IsLIntegrable (X n) μ := h₂
          have h₉ : IsLIntegrable (Y i) μ := by
            -- Use the fact that the conditional expectation of an integrable function is integrable.
            simpa [Y] using h₈  -- This is a placeholder and needs to be adjusted.
          exact h₉
        exact h₇
      exact h₅
    exact h₃
  -- Now we can show that the collection {Y i} is uniformly integrable.
  have h₂ : UniformIntegrable Y 1 μ := by
    sorry
  simpa [Y] using h₂

((Submartingale.martingale_iff_of_integrable_sq.exists (hX : Submartingale X 𝓕 μ))

have h₁ : UniformIntegrable (fun i ↦ (X n : Ω → ℝ)) 1 μ := by
    refine uniformIntegrable_const _ _ ⟨X n, _⟩
    exact hX.integrable n
  have h₂ : UniformIntegrable (fun i ↦ stoppedValue X (τ i)) 1 μ := by
    -- Use the fact that the submartingale is dominated by a uniform integrable family
    have h₃ : ∀ i, stoppedValue X (τ i) ≤ᵐ[μ] X n := by
      intro i
      have h₄ : ∀ ω : Ω, stoppedValue X (τ i) ω ≤ X n ω := by
        intro ω
        have h₅ : τ i ω ≤ n := hτ_le i ω
        have h₆ : stoppedValue X (τ i) ω ≤ X n ω := by
          -- Use the submartingale property to bound the stopped value
          have h₇ : IsLUB {k : WithTop ι | (τ i ω : WithTop ι) ≤ k} n := by
            -- Prove that n is the least upper bound of the set {k | τ i ω ≤ k}
            sorry
          -- Use the submartingale property to conclude the inequality
          sorry
        exact h₆
      exact h₄
    -- Conclude the uniform integrability using the domination
    sorry
  exact h₂

have hX' : UniformIntegrable (fun i => (X n : ι → Ω → ℝ)) 1 μ := by
    exact uniformIntegrable_const_1 (MemLp.one (hX.integrable n))
  have h₁ : UniformIntegrable (fun i => μ[X n | hτ i.measurableSpace]) 1 μ := by
    -- Use the fact that the conditional expectation of an integrable function is uniformly integrable
    refine' hX'.condExp' (fun i => by simpa using hτ i)
  -- Show that the stopped values are equal almost everywhere to the conditional expectations
  have h₂ : (fun i => stoppedValue X (τ i)) =ᵐ[μ] (fun i => μ[X n | hτ i.measurableSpace]) := by
    -- Prove that the stopped values are equal almost everywhere to the conditional expectations
    have h₃ : ∀ᵐ (ω : Ω) ∂μ, ∀ (i : ℕ), stoppedValue X (τ i) ω = μ[X n | hτ i.measurableSpace] ω := by
      filter_upwards [hX.ae_eq_condExp_of_isStoppingTime] with ω hω
      intro i
      have h₄ : ∀ (i : ℕ), stoppedValue X (τ i) ω = μ[X n | hτ i.measurableSpace] ω := by
        intro i
        have h₅ : stoppedValue X (τ i) ω = μ[X n | hτ i.measurableSpace] ω := by
          have h₆ : τ i ω ≤ n := hτ_le i ω
          have h₇ : IsStoppingTime 𝓟 (τ i) := hτ i
          have h₈ : stoppedValue X (τ i) ω = μ[X n | hτ i.measurableSpace] ω := by
            have h₉ := hX.ae_eq_condExp_of_isStoppingTime (hτ i) (hτ_le i)
            -- Use the fact that the stopped value is equal to the conditional expectation
            have h₁₀ : stoppedValue X (τ i) =ᵐ[μ] μ[X n | hτ i.measurableSpace] := by
              filter_upwards [h₉] with ω hω
              exact hω
            have h₁₁ : stoppedValue X (τ i) ω = μ[X n | hτ i.measurableSpace] ω := by
              apply h₁₀.ae
            exact h₁₁
          exact h₈
        exact h₅
      exact h₄ i
    -- Use the fact that the stopped values are equal almost everywhere to the conditional expectations
    have h₄ : (fun i => stoppedValue X (τ i)) =ᵐ[μ] (fun i => μ[X n | hτ i.measurableSpace]) := by
      filter_upwards [h₃] with ω hω
      simp_all [Function.funext_iff]
      <;> aesop
    exact h₄
  -- Use the fact that the stopped values are equal almost everywhere to the conditional expectations
  have h₃ : UniformIntegrable (fun i => stoppedValue X (τ i)) 1 μ := by
    have h₄ : UniformIntegrable (fun i => μ[X n | hτ i.measurableSpace]) 1 μ := h₁
    have h₅ : (fun i => stoppedValue X (τ i)) =ᵐ[μ] (fun i => μ[X n | hτ i.measurableSpace]) := h₂
    exact h₄.of_ae_eq h₅
  exact h₃
